metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `model_settings["timeout"] = cfg.timeout_s` must be set on the shared plain-dict path (after L268, alongside the existing `max_tokens` assignment at L277) — NOT inside `_anthropic_cache_settings` or any Anthropic-gated branch — so the timeout applies to all providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, local).
findings.finding: The plan claims timeout should be wired 'where runner.py already assembles the AnthropicModelSettings (alongside max_tokens / cache flags)', but the code shows AnthropicModelSettings is only constructed for the Anthropic provider path (inside `_anthropic_cache_settings`, which returns None for any non-Anthropic model). The `model_settings` dict that actually flows into `kwargs["model_settings"]` is assembled from the dict-conversion of cache_settings (or an empty dict), with `max_tokens` added separately. If an implementer follows the mechanism description literally and adds `timeout` inside `_anthropic_cache_settings` or the Anthropic-gated branch, non-Anthropic providers (OpenAI, Gemini) will silently not receive the timeout — the knob stays dead for those providers.
findings.location: Plan section 'API surface (grounded)' and 'Scope: In: src/rebar/llm/runner.py (set model_settings["timeout"] = cfg.timeout_s)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope/implementation note, replace 'where runner.py already assembles the AnthropicModelSettings (alongside max_tokens / cache flags)' with 'on the shared model_settings dict (alongside the existing max_tokens assignment at L277, after the cache_settings dict-conversion at L268) — not inside _anthropic_cache_settings, so it applies to all providers'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or a clearly labeled stretch/optional AC) that exercises the timeout end-to-end against the live Anthropic API — e.g. a test that sets a sub-second timeout via REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT and asserts the call raises a timeout/deadline error — OR explicitly defer this to a named child ticket with a rationale and document why the offline test is sufficient for this scope.
findings.finding: The ACs rely entirely on offline/mocked tests (monkeypatched Agent or inspection) to prove cfg.timeout_s reaches model_settings. No AC exercises the wired timeout end-to-end against the live Anthropic HTTP layer, so it is possible to satisfy every AC while pydantic-ai silently ignores the model_settings["timeout"] key in the actual HTTP call (e.g. if the key mapping differs between model paths). E6(f) applies because the default-flip makes every production LLM call use the new code path, and the anti-FP exceptions (opt-in flag, no live boundary, named-child deferral) do not apply.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('via inspection or a monkeypatched Agent capturing the settings')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to ACs: '(Optional/integration) With REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT=1 and a real model call, the runner raises a timeout exception within ~2 s — verifiable via `pytest -m integration -k test_timeout_enforced_live` (skipped in CI without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).' Mark it skip-in-CI-without-key so it does not block `make test` but is runnable locally and in integration environments.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (and corresponding implementation note) that a timeout exception is caught, logged at WARN/ERROR with `timeout_s` value included, and re-raised (or surfaced as a structured error), so operators can observe when the knob is actively bounding calls.
findings.finding: When the now-wired timeout fires (e.g., an operator sets REBAR_LLM_TIMEOUT tighter than 600 s), the resulting exception is neither logged, metered, nor traced by anything the plan specifies. Operators who experience unexpected hangs-that-are-now-timeouts will have no signal distinguishing a timeout from other LLM errors, defeating the diagnostic value of the fix.
findings.suggested_fix: In runner.py, wrap the `agent.run_sync(...)` call in a try/except for the timeout exception type (e.g., `httpx.TimeoutException` or pydantic-ai's equivalent), emit a structured log line such as `logger.warning('LLM call timed out', timeout_s=cfg.timeout_s, model=...)`, then re-raise. Add an AC: 'When a call exceeds timeout_s, a WARN-level log entry including the configured timeout_s value is emitted before the exception propagates.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
